content: Do you know why the Earth is getting warmer? It's because of something called greenhouse gases. These gases are like a big blanket that wraps around the Earth, keeping it warm. They come from things like cars, factories, and even our homes. When we burn fossil fuels, like gasoline or coal, it releases bad gases into the air. These gases trap heat from the sun, making the Earth warmer. Imagine you're in a car on a sunny day, and you roll up the windows. It gets really hot inside, right? That's kind of like what's happening with the Earth. The greenhouse gases are trapping heat, making the planet get warmer and warmer. But don't worry, we can all help to reduce these gases and keep the Earth cool. We can walk or bike instead of driving, turn off lights when not in use, and plant more trees to absorb the bad gases. Every small action we take can help make a big difference!
